heading: United States human space flight policy
label: 18311 United States human space flight policy
notes: CodificationIn subsec. Amendments2017—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “It is the policy of the United States that reliance upon and use of non-United States human space flight capabilities shall be undertaken only as a contingency in circumstances where no United States-owned and operated human space flight capability is available, operational, and certified for flight by appropriate Federal agencies.”Pub. L. 115–10

children.heading: Use of non-United States human space flight transportation services
children.label: a Use of non-United States human space flight transportation services
A
children.text: no United States Government-operated human space flight capability is available;

children.label: B
children.text: no United States commercial provider is available; and

children.label: C
children.text: it is a qualified foreign entity.

children.heading: Commercial provider
children.label: A Commercial provider
children.text: The term “commercial provider” means any person providing human space flight transportation services, primary control of which is held by persons other than the Federal Government, a State or local government, or a foreign government.

children.heading: Qualified foreign entity
children.label: B Qualified foreign entity
children.text: The term “qualified foreign entity” means a foreign entity that is in compliance with all applicable safety standards and is not prohibited from providing space transportation services under other law.

children.heading: United States commercial provider
children.label: C United States commercial provider
children.text: The term “United States commercial provider” means a commercial provider, organized under the laws of the United States or of a State, that is more than 50 percent owned by United States nationals.

children.heading: Arrangements with foreign entities
children.label: 3 Arrangements with foreign entities
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall prevent the Administrator from negotiating or entering into human space flight transportation arrangements with foreign entities to ensure safety of flight and continued ISS operations.

children.heading: United States human space flight capabilities
children.label: b United States human space flight capabilities
children.text: Congress reaffirms the policy stated in , that the United States shall maintain an uninterrupted capability for human space flight and operations in low-Earth orbit, and beyond, as an essential instrument of national security and of the capacity to ensure continued United States participation and leadership in the exploration and utilization of space.section 70501(a) of title 51
